tldr.bestFor: Teams building multilingual training, internal comms, or avatar-led review workflows with formal delivery needs
tldr.notFor: Light users, prompt-only creators, or teams expecting unlimited generation, zero identity drift, or fully proven one-click automation
tldr.why: Official video assets show stronger evidence for proofreader review, voice controls, admin restrictions, and SCORM export than for broad Video Agent automation

miniTest.prompt: Create a 45-60 second multilingual training update with an avatar presenter. Include one translated scene, proofreader review, voice/phonetic correction, and export-ready delivery notes.

checklist.label: Primary workflow fit
checklist.value: Multilingual training / internal comms / LMS delivery

checklist.label: Review gate
checklist.value: Proofreader can edit wording, phonetics, voice, and glossary before final generation

checklist.label: Voice control
checklist.value: Check Voice Director, voice mirroring, per-scene override, and apply-all behavior

checklist.label: Formal delivery
checklist.value: SCORM export has version, hosting mode, and completion-threshold signals

checklist.label: Identity drift risk
checklist.value: Avoid regenerate-look changes mid-project; official material treats this as recurring boundary

checklist.label: Admin controls
checklist.value: Verify publish/download restrictions, public avatar exposure, and feature restrictions

checklist.label: Unproven convenience layer
checklist.value: Auto-SCORM live sync and Video Agent need hands-on validation before strong claims

miniTest.verdict: Use this as a source-backed workflow check, not a completed hands-on run. HeyGen looks strongest when review, voice control, governance, and SCORM-style delivery matter more than generic avatar generation.

useCases.title: Multilingual training review
useCases.why: Official material supports a translation-to-proofreader workflow where reviewers can adjust wording, phonetics, voice choice, and glossary mapping before final generation

useCases.title: Formal LMS delivery
useCases.why: SCORM is more than an export label here: the official workflow references version choice, hosting mode, and completion-threshold configuration

useCases.title: Voice-controlled presenter updates
useCases.why: Voice Director, voice mirroring, phonetic review, and per-scene overrides make HeyGen better suited to presenter consistency than simple script-to-video conversion

keyFeatures: Proofreader workflow can edit wording, phonetics, voice choice, and glossary mapping
Admin controls cover workspace access, publish/download restrictions, public avatar exposure, and feature restrictions
SCORM export includes delivery-layer settings such as version, hosting mode, and completion threshold
Voice Director supports preset tone, custom prompt, per-scene override, and apply-to-all controls
Avatar 5 is positioned for stable longer A-roll; Seedance 2.0 is positioned for shorter cinematic B-roll
Identity drift is a recurring boundary when regenerating avatar look mid-project
Free plan outputs include a watermark
Export resolution depends on plan tier (Free/Creator/Team)
Subscription cancellation takes effect end of billing cycle
Refunds are granted only in specific cases, at discretion
Free plan use is non-commercial per FAQ
No plan offers unlimited AvatarIV usage and credits

pros: Strong official evidence for proofreader-led localization review
Voice controls go deeper than basic emotion labels
SCORM delivery signals support formal training workflows
Admin controls are more concrete than a generic enterprise label
cons: Identity drift remains a structural risk when avatar looks are regenerated
Auto-SCORM live sync is described but not fully demonstrated in a real LMS flow
Video Agent automation remains weaker evidence than the review and delivery workflow
Complex final polish may still require an external editor
Credits expiring monthly can waste spend for light users
Refund policy is discretionary, not guaranteed

verdict.bottomLine: HeyGen is best for teams who need avatar-led videos for sales outreach, training, and localization. The trade-off is cost control: credits expire after 30 days, and refunds are discretionary. Free outputs are watermarked; higher export resolutions and commercial rights depend on your plan tier.

faqs.question: Do free users get a watermark on exports?
faqs.answer: Yes—Free users get watermarked output on generated content. This is stated in official help documentation.

faqs.question: What export resolution do plans support?
faqs.answer: Official pricing states Free exports at 720p, Creator up to 1080p, and Team up to 4K. Higher resolution availability depends on your subscribed plan.

faqs.question: Do credits roll over to the next month?
faqs.answer: Credits expire 30 days after they are issued, so rollover beyond that window is not supported. Plan billing and issuance timing can still matter for how long you can use them.

faqs.question: Is there any plan with unlimited AvatarIV usage?
faqs.answer: No—official guidance says there is no plan, including enterprise, with unlimited AvatarIV usage and credits. You should expect feature usage to be credit-limited.

faqs.question: When does cancellation take effect after I cancel?
faqs.answer: Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing cycle. You typically keep access through the remainder of that cycle.

faqs.question: Can I get a refund if I forgot to cancel?
faqs.answer: Refunds are evaluated only under specific circumstances and at the company's discretion. User reports: this can clash with expectations when renewals happen.

faqs.question: Can I use Free plan videos commercially?
faqs.answer: The FAQ indicates Free plan usage is non-commercial. Commercial rights for paid plans: Needs verification from official terms or help.

faqs.question: What file format do exports download as (MP4, etc.)?
faqs.answer: Export/download documentation needs verification for explicit file format wording. Treat any assumed format as unconfirmed until verified.
